description: Dust: earth ground fine enough to ride the air. Third member of the 'mu-' earth family after 'muila' and 'muphia', and the drought's version of what mud is to rain. On the road, on the shelf, in the light through a window.
sound_symbolism: 'mu' keeps the earth-family signature and 'he' is the dry whisper it moves with. Nothing in the word presses down.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'muhena rihe.' (The dust rises.) 'howeli muhena phelu.' (The wind holds dust.) Distinct from 'hesura' (ash), which came through fire.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Dust was read like an instrument: a traveler's approach on the road, a dry season's warning in the field, a house's account of time passing.
